checks.a_analysis: The output includes AI-generated captions and summaries for images and tables (e.g., 'Table containing a comment regarding Slide 5', 'Graphic illustrating the economic impact...'). These are permitted as per the 'Acceptable Changes' guidelines. No other fabricated text was found.

All URLs found in the output (e.g., broadbandmap.fcc.gov/home, nconemap.gov/pages/broadband, catalog.data.gov/dataset/) are present in the source document. No fabricated phone numbers or addresses were detected.

The conversion pipeline explicitly omitted a massive portion of the document. The JSON contains a placeholder stating: '18 sections omitted for token budget'. This results in the loss of approximately 50% of the source document's content, including critical slides like 'Who is the FGDC?' (p. 4), 'Summary of FGDC Duties' (p. 5), 'Maturity Assessment' (p. 9), and 'Vision 2035' details (pp. 31-35).
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 37 pages of content, including slides 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 22, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 31, 32, 33, 34, and 35.
checks.b_evidence_location: In the JSON structure after the section 'FGDC and the Geospatial Data Act'.

checks.a_analysis: On slide 12 ('Economic Value of Location'), the source document visually presents 6 key statistics in circular icons. The corresponding 'Economic Impact Statistics' table in the output only contains 4 data points (10% Growth, 649,000 Jobs, $690 Million, 77% Open). The statistics for '$73 Billion' and '$1.6 Trillion' were removed from the table structure and moved into an image caption, which significantly reduces the data content of the table element (33% reduction).
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 12 contains 6 circular stats: $73 Billion, $1.6 Trillion, 10% Growth, 649,000 Jobs, $690 Million, 77% Open.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Economic Impact Statistics' table contains only 4 items: 10% Growth, 649,000 Jobs, $690 Million, 77% Open.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'NSDI: A Digital Infrastructure for the Nation' (which contains slide 12 content).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While 18 entire sections are missing (captured in C3), the individual sections that are present generally contain the text from their respective source pages.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text within the processed sections accurately reflects the wording of the source PDF, accounting for minor OCR artifacts.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. Each block of text and visual element corresponds to its single occurrence in the original slides. Intentional repetitions present in the source (such as the summary statement at the bottom of slide 10 being similar to the first bullet point) are correctly captured as separate elements, and no accidental duplication by the conversion pipeline was found.

The heading hierarchy is logically structured and consistent throughout the document. Slide titles are generally tagged as Level 2 headings. Comment sections related to specific slides are appropriately nested using Level 3 headings (e.g., 'Slide 11' and 'Slide 13' comments). There are no skipped heading levels (e.g., jumping from H1 to H3), ensuring clear navigation for screen reader users.

The conversion pipeline successfully identified and structured several tables. The complex grid on Page 30 ('Draft NSDI Goals and Objectives') is perfectly rendered with correct header identification and row/column alignment. Infographic statistics on Page 12 were intelligently converted into a table format to preserve data relationships in a machine-readable way. Comment slides were also correctly transformed into two-column tables representing the metadata and comment text. All table structures faithfully represent the layout and meaning of the source material.

The reading order follows the sequential flow of the presentation slides. Information within each slide is presented in a logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right order. Comment slides, which are separate pages in the PDF source, are placed immediately following the slide content they refer to, which is the most accessible and logical placement for the converted output.

Lists are correctly identified and categorized as either 'ordered' or 'unordered' based on the source formatting. Numbered lists (e.g., on Pages 3, 17, 20) are preserved as ordered lists. Bulleted lists (e.g., on Pages 2, 18, 19) are preserved as unordered lists. The nested list structure on Page 17 ('Examples of Local, State and Federal Data') is accurately captured, maintaining the parent-child relationships between list items.

All URLs mentioned in the text of the source document (such as broadbandmap.fcc.gov and nconemap.gov on Page 18) are present in the output text. No fabricated links were added, and no existing URLs from the source appear to be missing or corrupted.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document maintains the logical flow and organization of the original slide deck. Slides are flattened into a single-column vertical layout, which is standard for responsive HTML. The thematic sections follow the sequence of the PDF pages accurately.
The grid 'Draft NSDI Goals and Objectives' from PDF page 30 is correctly rendered as an HTML table. The headers and cell contents are aligned, and the information is clearly readable and structured.

Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output and have been replaced by solid green placeholder blocks. This includes the grid of 32 agency logos (Slide 4), the US map and North Carolina report card (Slide 9), the infographic elements for economic value (Slide 12), and the broadband maps (Slide 18). These are functional images that convey specific information not fully captured in the surrounding text.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful images: 32 agency logos on page 4; a US map and 'North Carolina Report Card' on page 9; broadband maps on page 18.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML rendered output, these images are absent and replaced by large green rectangular blocks (e.g., under 'Who is the FGDC?', 'NSGIC Maturity Assessment', and 'NSDI In Action').
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: 'Who is the FGDC?' (Slide 4), 'NSGIC Maturity Assessment' (Slide 9), 'NSDI In Action Serving Citizens – North Carolina' (Slide 18)

checks.a_analysis: The bulleted list content for the 'Priority National Datasets' section is visually duplicated in the HTML output. The list appears once, followed by a green block, and then the exact same list repeats immediately before the next section header.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF page 31 (Slide 27) shows a single bulleted list of nine priority datasets (Parcels through Boundaries).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the heading 'Priority National Datasets' followed by the list, a green box, and then the exact same list repeats again before the 'Draft NSDI Goal 1: Governance' heading.
